title: scry
description:
Searchable GraphQL ↔ MCP bridge for AI agents. Connects to one or many
GraphQL endpoints, introspects each schema, builds a per-upstream SQLite
+ FTS5 + BM25 index, and exposes ten MCP tools an agent can call. v0.1.0
is live; v0.2 hardens deployment, audit, and credential handling for
real-world hosted-MCP fleet operations.

features.title: scry doctor diagnostic CLI
features.description:
One CLI subcommand that probes the local config and runtime
surface — for each configured upstream: reachability, introspection
mode (full / shallow / sdl-file), auth status, scope sanity for
clients.yml entries, audit-dir perms (0700 dir + 0600 files),
OTel exporter connectivity when configured. Single command, one
verdict per check, exit code != 0 when anything fails. Goal: halve
first-run support burden by giving operators a self-service
smoke test before they file an issue.

features.title: Audit log rotation
features.description:
JSONL audit chains grow unbounded today. Add --audit-max-size
(default 50MiB per session) and --audit-keep (default 5 archived
files). On rollover: rename <session>.jsonl → <session>.jsonl.1,
shift older files, open a new .jsonl. Critical: chain links must
survive rotation — last hash of .jsonl.1 = previous hash for the
first record of the new .jsonl. Without this, VerifyChain breaks
across rollover. Right shape for production where audit lives for
years but per-session files would otherwise grow into hundreds of
MiB.

features.title: Custom auth header schemes
features.description:
Today scry only emits `Authorization: Bearer <T>`. Many real
upstreams use `X-API-Key`, `Token <T>`, custom headers, or no
scheme prefix. Add per-server auth.header_name (default
"Authorization") + auth.scheme (default "Bearer"; empty string =
no prefix) to servers.yml + clients.yml. Wired through
upstream.Client.SetAuth so hot reload picks up changes. Unblocks
every upstream that doesn't follow the OAuth 2.0 Bearer
convention.

features.title: OS keychain token-ref scheme
features.description:
Add `keychain://service.account` token-ref scheme alongside env://,
file://, op://. Resolves via macOS Keychain, libsecret on Linux,
DPAPI on Windows using 99designs/keyring (or zalando/go-keyring —
pick during design). Falls back to file:// gracefully when no
keyring backend is available (CI, Docker, headless servers).
Operators with desktop installs get OS-protected credential
storage; CI keeps working without change. Documents the headless
fallback prominently.

features.title: Per-server rate limiting
features.description:
Fortify's middleware chain ships a rate-limiter; scry doesn't wire
it. Add per-server rate_limit.rps + rate_limit.burst to
servers.yml. Layer onto the existing fortify chain in
upstream.New(). Failure mode: rate-limit rejection surfaces as a
structured `rate_limited` error envelope with retry_after hint, so
agents back off cleanly instead of hammering the upstream.

features.title: Production deployment guide + manifests
features.description:
docs/deployment/{docker,systemd,kubernetes}.md with copy-pasteable
artefacts: multi-stage Dockerfile with distroless final image, a
systemd unit with hardening flags (PrivateTmp, ProtectSystem=strict,
NoNewPrivileges, ReadWritePaths only the audit dir), and a
Kubernetes Deployment + ConfigMap + Secret + Service + optional HPA.
Replaces the current "configure your own systemd unit" gap that
keeps adoption a one-off engineering exercise per ops team.

features.title: Adopt mcp-go
features.description:
Filed mcp-go #90 proposes a first-party ToolFilter middleware that
filters tools/list responses by identity-aware predicates. When the
upstream PR merges + releases: replace scry's internal
tool_filter.go wrapper with the upstream-provided API. If still
pending at v0.2 cut: ship a PR to mcp-go ourselves rather than
carrying the workaround. Eliminates the schema-coupling concern
we already documented in the issue.

features.title: Race-stress integration test
features.description:
Existing tests run with -race but at low concurrency. Add a
build-tagged stress test (`-tags=stress`) that drives hot-reload +
Gate.Record + concurrent query_execute under a wrk-style load
generator for 30s. Goal: surface lock-ordering bugs and
hot-reload-during-write races that single-thread unit tests miss.
Run nightly in CI with a dedicated job so it gates merges only
when intentionally triggered.
